title: SQL parser has problem with (position of) a scalar subquery in a SELECT-list
poc: CREATE TABLE t2 (a int, b int);
SELECT b, (SELECT count(*) FROM t2 AS x WHERE x.a < t2.a), CASE a WHEN b THEN 444 END FROM t2;
SELECT b as "b1", (SELECT count(*) FROM t2 AS x WHERE x.a < t2.a), CASE a WHEN b THEN 444 END FROM t2;
SELECT b, CASE a WHEN b THEN 444 END, (SELECT count(*) FROM t2 AS x WHERE x.a < t2.a) FROM t2;
key_bug_features: Scalar subquery in SELECT-list causes SQL parser issue
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3 (id int PRIMARY KEY, name varchar(50), value float, created_at timestamp DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t3 AS x WHERE x.value > t3.value), CASE WHEN created_at IS NULL THEN 'No Date' ELSE 'Has Date' END FROM t3;
SELECT name AS "name_alias", (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t3 AS x WHERE x.id < t3.id), CASE value WHEN 20.0 THEN 'Matched' END FROM t3;
SELECT value, CASE WHEN value < 15.0 THEN 'Low' ELSE 'High' END, (SELECT AVG(value) FROM t3) FROM t3;
